title: [lichen_l2.c:441] lichen_peer_remove void return hides errors
description: Function: lichen_peer_remove. Contract violation: The function returns void but can fail in multiple ways: NULL eui64 (line 444), lora_l2_needs_reinit() (lines 457-460), or peer not found (silent). Callers cannot distinguish between 'peer removed' and 'removal failed due to aborted state'. The peer_add() counterpart returns int with error codes. Consider returning int for symmetry: 0 on success, -EINVAL for NULL, -ECANCELED for needs_reinit, -ENOENT if peer not found. Priority 3 because current callers don't check the result.
close_reason: Fixed: Changed lichen_peer_remove return type from void to int. Now returns: 0 on success, -EINVAL for NULL eui64, -ENOENT if peer not found, -ECANCELED if LoRa L2 needs reinit, -ENOTSUP if link support disabled.
